reasoning: The market close price of IPG will track Omnicom (OMC) tightly since Omnicom is acquiring IPG in an all-stock deal (0.344 OMC shares per IPG share), scheduled to close in Q4 2025. The period in question is a 1-week interval (Nov 7 close to Nov 14 close). For a typical stock, the probability of a positive return over a 1-week period is approximately 52-53% due to the equity risk premium. Neither IPG nor OMC are expected to report earnings or go ex-dividend during this specific one-week window. The narrow M&A arbitrage spread implies IPG might have a very tiny upward drift relative to OMC as closing approaches, but essentially this reduces to the baseline probability of OMC's stock going up over one week, which is squarely around 52%.
